rationale: Title X is more journal-ready because it is shorter, cleaner, and easier to scan while still conveying the intervention comparison, population, major finding, and study design. Title Y adds renal consequences, which improves specificity, but the phrase structure is crowded and less elegant for an HR title.
loser_weakness: Title Y is more informative, but it feels overpacked and syntactically heavier, especially with “Its Thromboinflammatory and Renal Consequences” before the ablation comparison.
